title: Bring up LilyGO GNSS, time, battery, and power services
description: Why this exists: location, time, battery, and power behavior are common sources of board-specific leakage. What needs to be done: validate GNSS UART/I2C if present, PPS/time source if present, battery ADC or PMIC telemetry, charging status, power-hold/shutdown controls, wake buttons, and any external flash/storage. Integrate through firmware-wide location/date-time providers and HAL capability discovery rather than board-specific app logic. Acceptance criteria: each supported service reports present/unavailable correctly; GNSS/time feed into provider abstractions where available; battery/power state appears in status resources/UI; unsupported or uncertain services have follow-up Beads.
notes: Design input from user on 2026-07-02: consider embedding build-time timestamps in firmware and/or board metadata as an epoch hint for GNSS/time bring-up. Treat as a lower-bound/warm-start source for time validity checks and stale-fix rejection, not as authoritative wall-clock time; no-hardware design/tests can cover build-epoch plumbing, while real GNSS lock validation remains hardware-blocked.
